private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
POLARIS Raumflugzeuge is a Series A–stage space and aerospace company founded by Alexander Kopp. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five private peers at the same or adjacent funding stage, including Aalyria ($1.3B, Series B), Stoke Space ($1.0B, Series B), ClearSpace ($400M, Series A), Hubble Network ($400M, Series B), and Sift ($274M, Series B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given the lack of disclosed revenue. The median valuation across the five comparables is $400M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (45.3x median across six Space & Aerospace tickers including RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, and SATL) cannot be applied without the target's revenue figures, a common constraint with private companies. The peer-median result therefore carries full analytical weight.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, with a central point of $400M. This reflects POLARIS's positioning within an early-stage, capital-intensive sector where Stage B peers command significant premiums. Key uncertainties center on the company's differentiated technical approach relative to the median peer, capital efficiency relative to peers, and revenue traction not visible in this dataset. Validation of the median against stage-specific funding dynamics and technology maturity would strengthen conviction in this range.
